title: The Throne
description: The palace. King Aldric on his throne. A moment of genuine contact before the performance closes. Critical setup for the Rogue's choice against Mordaine.

lines: The palace was a dungeon that didn't know it was a dungeon.
Every corridor was a test. Every room had a purpose beyond its decoration. The guards fought in patterns — not because they were disciplined, but because they couldn't do otherwise.
The tapestries on the walls told the kingdom's history. Examined closely, the history had been edited. A consistent hand. A single revising eye.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Someone has been here before us. Not recently. Over years. Adjusting things.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The same hand as the chart.

steps.text: The throne room was at the end of everything. King Aldric sat on his throne. He looked like a painting of a king — positioned correctly, lit properly, expression carefully neutral.
When the party entered, his eyes moved. And for a moment — brief, unmistakable — something alive flickered behind the performance.

lines.speaker: King Aldric
lines.text: She's trying to help. The kingdom was failing. She—

steps.text: The words came out rote. Policy-shaped. He heard them as he said them. Something in his face shifted — not anger, but awareness of the performance he was giving.

lines.speaker: King Aldric
lines.text: I know what she's done. I know and I cannot—

steps.text: His hand moved to his chest. The gesture was not theatrical. It was the motion of a man checking whether something was still there.
The performance resumed. His eyes went neutral. The king who had been briefly present retreated behind the king who was a process.
The Cleric stepped forward. She didn't speak. She pressed her hand over the king's, where his had rested against his heart. A small, quiet gesture. The kind that costs nothing and means everything.
Something like gratitude moved across his face before the policy returned.
The Rogue said nothing else. But something had changed in the set of her shoulders. The kind of change that happens when a decision you didn't know you were making quietly completes itself.
